pipesnphysics.gui.goggles.head: Head
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_1: A fluid tank filled with water
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_2: We want to move the fluid to this empty tank
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_4: Fluid won't move on its own through horizontal pipes. It needs a pump!
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_5: A Mechanical Pump creates pressure to push fluids through pipes
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_6: Give it rotational power and the pump starts pushing fluid through the network
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_7: The pump pushes fluid to all connected pipes in its network
pipesnphysics.ponder.pump.text_8: The pump's facing direction determines which way it pushes
pipesnphysics.ponder.friction_new.text_1: Pushing fluid through pipes creates friction
pipesnphysics.ponder.friction_new.text_2: Each horizontal pipe segment costs friction, reducing range and flow
pipesnphysics.ponder.friction_new.text_3: Vertical pipes have zero friction. Gravity does the work for free!
pipesnphysics.ponder.friction_new.text_4: Each horizontal pipe segment has a friction value (configurable in settings)
pipesnphysics.ponder.pressure_2.text_1: Pressure is the force that pushes fluid through pipes, overcoming friction
pipesnphysics.ponder.pressure_2.text_2: A vertical drop creates pressure. Each block of height adds to it
pipesnphysics.ponder.pressure_2.text_3: A mechanical pump also creates pressure, driven by RPM from your kinetic network
pipesnphysics.ponder.pressure_2.text_4: As long as pressure exceeds friction, fluid flows. Use goggles to see the breakdown!
pipesnphysics.ponder.l_shape_drop.text_1: This tank sits high up, building pressure from the vertical drop
pipesnphysics.ponder.l_shape_drop.text_2: The vertical pipes add zero friction, all the pressure is preserved
pipesnphysics.ponder.l_shape_drop.text_3: The horizontal pipes spend that pressure on friction, one segment at a time
pipesnphysics.ponder.l_shape_drop.text_4: A taller drop means more pressure, letting fluid push further horizontally
pipesnphysics.ponder.uphill.text_1: A tank filled with fluid at ground level
pipesnphysics.ponder.uphill.text_2: This pump needs to push fluid upward through vertical pipes
pipesnphysics.ponder.uphill.text_3: Each block of height costs pressure, gravity works against the pump
pipesnphysics.ponder.uphill.text_4: A faster pump (more RPM) can push fluid higher
pipesnphysics.ponder.uphill.text_5: Pushing downhill is free, gravity assists instead of fighting
